clinic: CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health)

reviews.text:
I am writing this review to warn others who may be considering CRGH. Unfortunately, my experience has been deeply disappointing and distressing.

1. Incorrect stimulation protocol (rookie mistake)
My consultant (Jara Ben Nagi) prescribed an inappropriate stimulation dose despite having full access to my previous IVF protocol. In my earlier cycle at another clinic, I was given 375 units, which resulted in 4 blastocysts.
At CRGH, I was initially prescribed 325 units, which was only increased to 375 units after 3 days, when it was already too late. This cycle resulted in just 2 blastocysts.
When I challenged my consultant, she admitted she could have prescribed a higher dose from the start. Correct stimulation dosing is basic IVF care, and this mistake should not have happened.

2. Lack of transparency during egg collection
Egg collection was performed by another consultant (Efsthatios Theodorou) . After the procedure, I was told that 6 mature eggs were collected, which was shocking given that 17 follicles were visible on ultrasound.
When I asked how many eggs were collected in total, both consultants said “all” — yet were unable to provide a number. To this day, I still do not know how many eggs were actually retrieved.
The explanation provided was confusing and inconsistent, and even the embryology team could not confirm the total number. This lack of transparency is extremely concerning, especially as my previous IVF cycle at another clinic resulted in 11 eggs collected, 10 of which were mature.

3. Poor clinical support during the implantation phase
I have an underactive thyroid and take levothyroxine, with regular monitoring through my GP. Before transfer, my TSH was below 2.5. After transfer, it increased to 3.3, which can negatively affect implantation.
I contacted the clinic in a state of genuine panic and shared my blood results via screenshots from my NHS app, explaining that an official GP letter would take time. I urgently asked whether my medication dose needed adjusting.
A nurse called Anna refused to escalate the issue due to the format of the results. Only after I was extremely distressed — shouting and close to tears — did she agree to ask a doctor for advice and promised a same-day call. I am still waiting for that call. Why my concern and extreme worry wasn’t priority but following a procedure to the point of absurdity is?

4. My Consultant left without notification
During this same call, I was told — for the first time — that my consultant had left the clinic. No one informed me of this beforehand, nor was I told who my new consultant is, despite the fact that I am still in the implantation phase. This is completely unacceptable.

5. Extremely expensive and not worth it
CRGH is a very expensive clinic, and based on my experience, it is absolutely not worth the cost. Poor communication, questionable clinical decisions, lack of transparency, and inadequate patient support made an already stressful IVF journey far worse.

Do not use this clinic.
If you are deciding between clinics or just starting your fertility journey, I strongly recommend looking elsewhere.

*Do not delete
Please believe all the negative reviews. This place is the worst choice for IVF in the UK, we had a terrible experience from start to finish, rude staff, wrong …

1 star is for the technical teams such as nurses and embryologists who are kind,  informative and professional.... the rest of the organisation is a total mess! I would NOT recommend coming here, ever. They are disorganised, dont communicate, misplace information, dont answer emails, and dont call back as promised. It adds a level of stress and emotional strain for a process that is already likely to be challenging. I have cried with frustration and distress just trying to communicate with their Finance department. The sudden closure of their city office and transition to GPS has exacerbated an already disfunctional practice. If I had choice, I would not trust this organisation with such an important process! However I've already paid upfront for treatment, so now I'm stuck. Note: this review is not a reflection of the outcome of treatment... we haven't even got that far yet! We havent even got to the 'emotional part' of treatment and I've wanted to give up and not proceed so many times already out of sheer frustration and distress working with this commercial yet unprofessional organisation.
I feel completely misled by my experience at CRGH and do not recommend this clinic. …

The experience with this clinic has been frustrating from start to finish. Booking appointments is an absolute nightmare — there’s constant confusion about availability, and it often feels like no one communicates internally. The miscommunication between the finance and admin teams has been especially stressful, leading to repeated billing errors and unclear charges.

Customer service is another major issue. It’s nearly impossible to get timely responses, and when you do, the staff often seem disorganised and unhelpful. For a clinic dealing with sensitive and important matters, the lack of professionalism is really disheartening.
I had a positive outcome with city! Though awful service and being hounded by debt collectors for payments that are not due! Can’t ever get hold of anyone. …

The Crgh itself is beautiful and has a great atmosphere, but my experience was completely spoiled by the attitude of one staff member, Zahava Edelstein. She was rude and unhelpful, which made the visit very unpleasant. It’s disappointing because the place has so much potential — I hope management addresses the issue and improves customer service.”

Very Disappointing Experience as an Overseas Patient – Poor Communication and Post-Care Negligence
I came to CRGH London as an overseas patient, hoping for professional care and …

I had a very disappointing experience at CRGH City Clinic, specifically with Dr. Javier Cordero. The ultrasound follicle tracking consultation lasted less than one minute, with no proper measurements taken — only vague and inconsistent follicle counts that were later changed without explanation.

To verify, I went to another clinic the next day, where I received a thorough and professional scan with completely different results.

On top of that, I paid for three blood test metrics but later discovered that only the AMH test was done — something the clinic never disclosed until I was contacted by their financial department.

While CRGH eventually refunded all my payments after I raised a formal complaint, the time and trust lost cannot be recovered. Worse, no one explained how they plan to address these serious quality issues.

I cannot recommend this clinic. The service I received was far below the standards expected from an accredited fertility clinic, and such carelessness can lead to serious consequences for patients seeking treatment.

Deeply Disappointed with CRGH – Please Avoid If You Can

CRGH lacks a personal touch. Receptionists—who aren’t medically trained—ask …

Sadly we had a bad pre-treatment experience with CRGH which made us choose another clinic. They had a special offer for a free consultation, so we tried to book one and a rude sales person called to interrogate me about why we wanted the …
Why is a visibly ill (likely with the flu) receptionist working? People may need to cancel cycles if they get ill. Please do better!

The worst experience of any clinic I have been to. Challenges run from being sent multiple contradicting plans to being sent unexpected invoices the day before appointments and threatened with cancelling treatments if those invoices are not paid. From being sent private medical details of other patients to having to project manage the process myself because departments don’t speak to each other. I’ve just received a letter for payment years after using them with no idea what it’s for and with no communication for many years about anything outstanding.

If you raise any concerns about their process it is never acknowledged it is their fault. Look through trust pilot rather than google for what appears to be honest reviews. Another great resource is glassdoor if you want to get the employee’s perspective.

I had 5 rounds of egg retrieval procedures done in the past. 2 at another clinic in the UK and 2 overseas. I had one of the most painful egg retrieval experiences recently at CRGH great portland street. Not sure why nobody mentions this. Maybe we ladies are too good at bearing pain.
Unfortunately I have to give this clinic 1 star- they do not deserve it. It’s taken me 8/9 months to write this review after I dealt with this horrendous clinic. …

If i could give 0 starts I would. This place is awful and I seriously do not recommend for anyone.
It has taken me 3 years to find the courage to write this review.
They do not have any pre or after care.
This place has left me mentally, emotionally and even physically distraught.
First, they bruised both my arms with all the trial and error blood tests they were trying to take. Second, when things don't go to plan there is no support no explanation and certainly no aftercare. Third, they only talk to you when they need an invoice paid. I asked several times to speak to someone about what happens next and everytime I was told I'd get a call back. I am still waiting 3years for a call back!
I do not recommend anyone to come and waste their money or their hope here.

This review is of the finance department and not of the clinic itself, which we’ve had a generally good experience with - especially with Dr Cardenas, I can’t recommend her enough, and all the nursing, sonographers and other hands on staff, …

DO NOT USE. Please women, do not use this centre. It has taken me so long to take the courage to speak out about this place. It is a battery farm. The doctors do not have expertise gynae knowledge, the after care is poor. If a woman dies during child labour and dies, but delivery of the child is successful, is their benchmark of success- as that analogy was how they saw my case, that my extraction was successful.

CRGH failed to give the right medication, monitor my situation properly and I was rushed to A&E post procedure, suffered OHSS and now potentially unable to conceive or even carry a child naturally, because of the damage caused by CRGH doctors. Due to the amount I've suffered - it has involved so much care - and it has turned out that most gynae's know not to use CRGH. I've also come across other women who've suffered, but have been too traumatised to talk about it.

N.B Please note that I have made lots of attempts to speak to the clinic, and that was even more traumatic- where every attempt was made to dismiss the negligence.

I continue to be disappointed with my experience of egg freezing at CRGH. I went with them as they said that before and after work scans and appointments would be available. They were not. I could not get anyone I had contact with to give …

UPDATED: CRGH, you have not even had the courtesy to respond to my review. I would appreciate some response.
Firstly, please DO NOT be swayed by all the positive reviews. They probably …

My partner and I were really disappointed by the clinic especially the quality of service we received. A gynaecologist recommended this clinic and after some research about success rate we were comfortable using them. However, the staff …

Having done lots of research into private clinics we went with CRGH based on their strong statistics. We did 2 rounds of IVF and and IUI after being told that after our 2 cycles of IVf had failed there was nothing more that could be done. The consultant himself was very nice and professional and i got the feeling that he felt let by  the other staff at times. I was informed i had endometriosis  by a Sonographer after  a HYCosy Scan. She shrugged her shoulders when i asked what was going on and why this had only been picked up at that time and she replied she assumed i knew. Clearly it was. shock and delivered in a very unsympathetic way. I feel that this has been the main issue with my infertility and this should have been looked into before starting treatment to rule it out. No pelvic exams or questions were asked about possible problems before treatment started. Its really the doctor that should give you diagnosis and reassure you. I was told there was a chance it could be cancer and naturally panicked after being told by a nurse that i should seek an MRI scan elsewhere as my symptoms were no longer their problem. Overall the service was shabby and we lost a lot of faith and trust in how things were being run. We were faced with huge disappointments but we should have been reassured at regular stages as the process is not cheap. Having left the clinic disheartened and angry at the way things were handled we have now found another clinic which have given us hope and made us feel it is not the end of the road for our fertility.

The consultant we saw at CRGH misread some of our notes and gave us incorrect advice. The consultant also claimed to be a specialist in low AMH levels and follicle count but the advice we were given was substandard compared to other …

Rude…. I rang up as a new patient enquiry. I required a specific cost, I was told that no one in that department was currently available to answer that question at the moment. I asked if they could call me back with the cost. I was told no and asked to ring back in 20minutes. I politely said that I had requested that they call me back when they had the information that I needed. The person on the phone went completely silent, and needless to say I have had no phone call back. I have called another clinic in London who were incredibly helpful. Seems that new patients aren’t important to this clinic.

CAVEAT: I have not included my name as I wish for my treatment to be kept private - however I would be v happy for CRGH to leave a message should they wish to know my full name in order to prove that I was a genuine patient until recently, …

Edit (27.08.2023):
I'm editing in light of CRGH asking me to contact them. Don't be fooled by this token response. I tried to raise concerns  a few times and they had no interest in what I had to say. All they care about is making money. Our consultant had no interest in doing what was best for us. We were kept in the dark about treatment decisions (despite us asking) and were told we couldn't speak to a doctor whenever we asked to. I'm so angry and upset.

Original review:
I am writing this review as someone who had a few cycles at CRGH. The clinic caused me nothing but stress.  The main problems were finance, nursing and admin.

The finance department at CRGH are unprofessional, dishonest and incompetent. They caused me a significant amount of stress at a time when I was meant to be minimsing stress.

A few examples of what they did:
- Overcharged me many times (this was normal practice).
- Regularly and aggressively harassed me for payments I had already made.
- Charged me for procedures I did not have.
- Quoted me the most expensive prices for tests when cheaper options were available.
- Said I couldn't proceed with an appointment until I had paid money I did not even owe.
- Quoted me one price and invoiced for a higher price, saying the prices had gone up.
- Regularly sent me invoices late and expected payment within two days.
- Never apologised for their mistakes, despite making errors regularly. They sometimes even tried to gaslight me and pretend they hadn't made a mistake.
- Conveniently ignored my messages querying costs.

Please please check every single item on every single invoice. Do not give finance your credit card details and do not authorise them to take payments at their will. You will regret this.

CRGH are very opaque about their pricing and you end up paying A LOT more than you originally think you will.  There's always added extras that crop up.

I should not have had to spend my time arguing with finance on the phone and trying to get refunds or credits applied but that is what I had to do. There seems to be no standards in the finance team at all. They had a significant negative impact on my mental health. Hardly what you need when undergoing a stressful process.

Nursing staff will tell you that many patients complain about the finance team but they just wash their hands off it and do nothing to help. These nurses are regulated by the NMC. How does being complicit in this unethical practice fit with the NMC professional standards of practice? Being a nurse means behaving ethically and challenging poor practice.

You will see from many reviews that the finance team's unethical practice is commonplace.  However, CRGH do nothing about it. This makes me think that overcharging is deliberate practice (in the hope that patients will not notice).

Some of the nursing staff are abrupt, uncaring and rude. I have no idea why they're working in fertility given they have no empathy.  They were constantly making mistakes and never apologised or took accountability.  They are completely disorganised and I had to point out their mistakes several times (e.g. prescription errors, not knowing my treatment plan, not responding to portal messages). They also seemed uninterested in my questions and concerns. They act like patients are an inconvenience to them.

Your consultant does not oversee your treatment and decisions are made by different doctors each time you have a scan. What is the point of having a named consultant if they do not oversee your treatment? It left me feeling mistrustful, in the dark and confused. This is not ok for the amount of money I paid.

When I used to call up, I would invariably end up speaking to admin staff who were patronising, rude and obstructive. It's almost as if they are trying to put obstacles in the way.

CRGH have no interest in accepting feedback and learning from it. Whenever I raised concerns, nothing ever changed and I just got fobbed off or gaslit. They just close ranks and make out you are the problem. This speaks volumes about what they really think of patients.

Do not expect to be taken care of properly. Even for medication you are the one who supposed to be knowing what doses and names your medicine is! Nurses and scan specialists are so nice and friendly they care and they are gentle, treat …

We started treatment with CRGH with lots of hope after a failed cycle in NHS.  The doctor asked us to redo lots of test (though we had done this a year back) and we did everything and spent a fortune
hoping there will be some positive output.  After all the investigation tests and history of tests from NHS, the doctor prepared a worst plan for Egg collection and the medicines suggested is also without any logic.  The follicles were not not growing as expected and we asked the hospital to cancel the cycle as we did not expect many mature eggs (basic logic and we were correct, not sure what the doctors logic was).
The doctor did not cancel the cycle and that means we paid a fortune for treatment and then as expected, there was only 2 average Embryo freezed (in NHS cycle we had 4 atleast).  On the embryo transfer day, we asked for both the embryos to be transferred and the embryologists prepared only one and the excuse she gave was lame and she prepared one more in the last minute. When the doctor transferred the embryo itself, I knew this wont be successful as he itself was not confident
and the blabbering he did was a proof (the NHS embryo transfer was smooth, but unfortunately miscarried).

I did not even know the logic of CRGH asking us to do multiple Covid 19 test when we were not even visiting the hospital and there was no symptoms also.  Anyway, we lost around 20K GBP for a BFN and what we feel is CRGH is only trying to extract money and no service at all.

Update:  I expected this standard template reply from the hospital, however the whole process was messed up big time and nothing was done correctly except sending invoices / debiting money on time.

Had a horrible experience that has left us scarred mentally and physically, as an overseas patient the neglect and lack of proper guidance and medical care was apathetic, would not recommend any overseas patient to consult this clinic, the …

Although I always read reviews, this is one of the first I have written. I did a huge amount of research before I chose a clinic, reading various reviews from many places.

I chose CRGH because I met a consultant there who seemed professional and knowledgeable. I started the process and very soon I realised how impersonal and factory like the place was. Unfortunately they made a few mistakes with me and I started to loose trust. They accidentally changed my appointments without me knowing and changed my consultant in the middle saying I had requested it, which I had not. There were also various hidden costs, such as the price of a test does not include the results for which you have to pay another amount to receive! The finance team are very active. The consultant herself was cold and impersonal, and in one confidential phone meeting with her, which she insisted must be on loud speaker, she didn’t let me know that there were other staff in the room until I questioned it.

I kept going as I had already started and didn’t want to go elsewhere in the middle. Unfortunately in the middle I was diagnosed with a condition and the consultant Dr S actually shouted at me as to why I had not told her before and why I had ‘withheld’ information. I told her that I had no idea I had this condition and it had only come up in a recent blood test. She was abrupt and challenging and more concerned about legally covering herself then acknowledging that it may be a shock to me too that I had just found out I had this condition. Though she was knowledgeable and experienced, she was cold, unfriendly and robotic and made me feel awful.

I tried to speak to the manager and he was very defensive that I was questioning a Consultant and put down the phone on me, saying he did not need to speak to me if he didn’t know who I was. Suffice to say I would never go back there again. Several of the nurses there were lovely as were the friendly receptionists, but if the manager himself is so rude, then if there is an issue, there is simply no-one to speak to who cares.

I wish everyone all the best in their journey and hope you find the right clinic and consultant for you.

Very expensive, not attentive, no one available to talk to.

We started to investigate with CRGH in Nov.2017. We had all the tests …

I had a negative experience and they were unwilling to address any of my concerns. Problems:
1:They don’t put anything in writing for you, (no appointment times, or notes from your scans, nothing tangible) so I had to take notes myself constantly. Even emails are electronically held in their system and you can only access them temporarily, then they disappear, so it’s hard to keep track of all the info you need to know.
2: There’s an awkward reception system where they take your photograph and there’s always a camera on you and yet it’s hard to get in through the main door from the high street
3: the consultant I met with has limited social skills. He puts his foot in it often with very sensitive topics. He only reviews your notes once the appointments have begun. He gives advice off the top of his head so you’re never sure whether it’s considered medical advice or a random idea he thinks might be fun to try.
4: sometimes I would have to call or email 3 times before someone would call me back
5: it’s very expensive and the costs are not clearly revealed until the invoices arrive
6: they prescribe medication that they will tell you is accessible in all pharmacies and health shops but then you will find the medication is actually only available directly from the clinic, at triple the cost
7: I was told the final and most important procedure - the egg collection - would be under general anaesthetic and pain free. Instead they changed their minds at the last minute so I was awake and in pain throughout the surgery. They did not offer additional pain relief at any point.
8: maybe all of the above is worth it because it is a success for some people and they are able to have children through this process. I don’t know if this is true though. It felt like a scam to me, because after all this, after many expensive months of scans and appointments and injections and attempts they did not manage to collect and freeze more than one egg. So overall had a very negative experience, and unsuccessful, and they were unwilling to address any of my post- procedure concerns.

EDIT TO ADD AS I'VE HAD A LOT OF MESSAGES ABOUT THIS REVIEW

Sadly CRGH never reached out to me again even after I emailed as …

Would not recommend especially if you are NHS patient. Unlike St Thomas or Queen Charlotte, if you have a problem such as needing fibroids removed before ivf, you will need to be referred back to your GP and face huge wait time. Consultants are very business like. Ivf remains crude and has not advanced much in the last 20 years, consultants give too postive perspectives to clients without really outlining the risks. On PDG, they are particualy bad, they don't tell patients that there is decreased pregnancy rates with PGD especially with age and people may actually be better off trying naturally. . This establishment is nurse lead (all of whom are nice), gaining access to consultants is very difficult however, and the attitude of some of the consultant are very poor. The establishment should have feedback system for each consultant after each consultation.

Disgraceful. Overrated. Unprofessional.
After two failed treatments (paid upfront before any procedures take place) and after 14 months when our last treatment took place, their idiotic Finance …

Booked in for couples fertility tests- staff is nice enough though quite dismissive, nurse was lovely. Unfortunately, this place has huge financial targets to meet & it shows. Understandably the bottom line is that patients want results, however, the brash, in-out manner employed is awful. They charge extortionate amounts and even so their manner leaves much to be desired. An assessment is just that and even though CRGH is a fertility clinic, there are various levels and options involved regarding fertility which need to be customised to each patients needs. Unfortunately CRGH fell short with this- no options, alternatives, or anything other than IVF we’re discussed. Biggest doozy though- even though results were good for both me & hubby,  Dr we saw for follow-up insisted we do IVF repeatedly, gave us no alternative options and embarrassingly the ultrasound, blood tests & Dr didn’t pick up that I was already pregnant (which I didn’t know at the time)!! Missed it completely!! Even with leading questions to the Dr at follow-up who just just interested in selling her IVF treatment!! We didn’t even bother telling her, her consultation was inadequate and she had no interest other than securing an IVF booking. As a medical precessional myself I expect higher standards. Not impressed!!

I do not recommend. They kept chasing us for money during a panademic when we had lost our jobs and our home was flooded and would not set up a payment plan unless we signed forms to get rid of our embryos. They never offered us counselling …

We had a really bad experience with CRGH and unfortunately we did several treatments with them. We didn't change clinic before because of the waiting times to do that. They are never on time once we had to wait for 5 hours and never less then one hour, disorganized, disrespectful, doctor didn't even bother to read our file. Not personalized at all. I would not recommend this clinic. Avoid if you can. There are better places in London.

I wish I would have chosen another place from start. There are too many issues and it becomes really frustrating to manage the hassle with clinic while you are also going through a sensitive time so I would really recommend to try somewhere …

We did not have the best experience with the clinic. We paid a lot of money for a test. We were told the results came out normal and we should try conceiving naturally. To find out over five moths later when booking another consultation with the doctor  that the result was quite poor and we can only conceive via IVF. We were more than puzzled with the advice. While we do not disagree with the fact that the doctor was very capable we found the clinic and the staff very disorganised.

Very disappointing experience with this clinic. Be aware!
I have just gone through one of the most traumatic experiences with a health clinic of my life. We spent over 10k with them and we have absolutely nothing to …

Came here for a free mini consult (15mins) in view of doing a much longer consult (which we were happy to pay £200), however upon arrival, the receptionist (who wore glasses), was very rude and her tone of voice was nasty. After a few …
Unpleasant experience from start to finish, compounded by incredibly rude credit control staff. Avoid.

Sadly despite getting a happy result, i cannot recommend crgh to anyone. i would recommend they need to take a serious look at the customer experience/customer journey as despite the people being very nice the processes involved are drawn out and frustrating. I allways had the feeling of being past from pillar to post with no one taking responsiblity, the response each time was oh i dont deal with that you need to speak to so and so. Getting the answers to my questions allways took a very long time and involved calling around different departments, being placed on hold, being told someone would call be back etc. Also for the prices they charge i would expect the clinic to be nicer.

Worst experience ever.... I do regret having treatment here.
I had an IUI back in August and I've just gone through an IVF cycle with a recent transfer, not sure about the results yet, however my overall experience has …

Beware - they changed their whole internal system, and it has been a disorganized mess. Everyone working there is confused about how their system/portal works. Appointments are delayed. People don't know what's going on. Whoever was in charge of this tech transition should be fired.

Terrible customer service. Medical procedures instill confidence and specialists are helpful and knowledgeable, but their nurses and reception are absolutely clueless. As an existing client I've been trying to schedule an appointment for ages now - they don't reply to emails or return calls. We've been overcharged /financial department is the only proactive one!/ And there's only one nurse actually capable of drawing blood - I've had my veins slaughtered a number of times before I invariably end up with their senior nurse.. I can see why they have a good success rate - they do a lot more screenings than other clinics and their specialists appear professional, but likely because of their reputation they don't feel they need to also deliver customer service so get ready for total chaos outside the doctor's office. We're already tied up with them too much to switch and have no complaints with the medical side of things but we're wondering how professional you can be medically if you can't get administration right - the impression is of chaos within the organisation and that's not trust instilling at all.

I have no words to describe how we were treated during our initial consultation and subsequent phone calls. We did not choose CRGH based on their poor customer service and insensitive and unprofessional consultant. Unfortunately they don't have a complaint procedure.

This is very bad service, they only care about charging money from the patient but discard the embryo while money has been paid. The doctor consulted the opposite advice while the storage department requires more consent form which is …

As lovely as the staff are I am
posting this to forewarn hopeful parents to be- please explore all possible avenues before embarking on breathtakingly expensive ivf. We were told we had a very …

I really couldn't comment on the services of this organisation as I didn't even get as far as booking an appointment - their staff made it impossible.  The telephone service here is quite atrocious and I am convinced I was speaking to children.  I made several attempts to book my initial consultation where I was constantly put on hold and cut off more than once, using up my entire phone credit.  When I did get through, and asked to book a consultation, I was met with a strange reply of "who with?" like I was expected to know the names of their consultants!  Do not phone and do not email, just go to their premises and ask to speak with its management because despite their ridiculous reception, the services they provide might actually be good.

APALLING SERVICE AVOID!!

Had to fill the same forms in multiple times as they always lost them. …

Just don’t.

Poor service and billed us twice.

Very oriented and no sympathy for your position.

Poor experience. Incorrect information shared, very transactional service. Not a warm clinic that felt supportive during a process that was a big one in our lives. When raised concerns and desires to go elsewhere they just wished us luck. No attempt to address concerns. Wouldn't recommend.

This clinic is a total joke.  Went in for an initial consulation, came out with a £2000 bill for "tests".  Shame they didn't mention any additional charges on the day of the consulation.  Admin team are useless. …

Very dissapoonted. My experience is terrible and i found the clinic lacking any degree of respect or empathy towards patients. The attitide is shocking and frankly very rude. I certainly will not be proceeding.
Made an enquiry and the contact centre made fun of my accent and they called me rude. They were laughing in my face and weren't accomodating.

They don’t provide the patient with enough information. They always in a hurry. And the emergency number and the desk number does not work during weekend. And if you want to contact the embryologist to ask a question you cannot get them. Very disappointed with the clinic. Will not recommend that clinic.

Do you get paid to play smooth radio and alllllllllllll the upbeat classics to anxious people who have paid expensive fertility treatment in your waiting room for appointments that are over 50 minutes late.

Wait for hours to speak to someone. Eventually get through to staff who are inexperienced. Seems money driven. Heard very negative things about this place. High staff turnover. Very badly managed. If you gonna spend money go somewhere where they actually care.

Really bad clinic, My wife and I spent thousands and they didn't even do an egg collection. I felt milked  and not looked after well enough for the money paid. Suggestion, look somewhere else for treatment.

Unfortunately a terrible experience. Avoid this place like the plague. CRGH only care about taking your money. Please read all the reviews carefully before proceeding. Unprofessional and full of lies.
Terrible customer service. Phone lines take too long to answer and voicemail inbox is full so you can’t leave a message.
Rude and incompetent patient liaison and nursing staff. A dreadful experience.

I chose CRGH for IVF treatment because of the clinic’s reputation for personalised treatment plans and experience with complex fertility cases. Having already undergone 6 egg collections and 3 embryo transfers abroad without success, I was …

I've been in touch with this clinic a number of times and have always regretted it. Although we have chosen not to have treatment with this clinic (because of the nature of the customer service) the attitude and response from the reception/admin team has never ceased to amaze me. It is quite frankly terrible especially considering how sensitive the subject matter is and people reaching out to this clinic at their most vulnerable time. I would recommend anyone to look at the Trust Pilot reviews before starting an uphill battle with this clinic.

My experience with this clinic was not positive. For over two months, we have tried to start to start the process without any success. The front line and administrative staff have made the experience taxing and difficult for the two of us. …

We had the worst experience with the clinic. The persons at the reception desk (one male by phone, one female in person) are not trained at all in costumer services, they have ZERO skills in a person-centered approach, their behavior is not professional and we felt that the service was more like a retail store instead of a high-end clinic. It is a shame because we are giving up the entire clinic without having the first appointment with the Dr., based on the experience we had from the administrative staff. We have been dealing with them for more than 2 months without any success in starting the fertility process. The breaking point was today when after obtaining an appointment for this week, we arrived to the clinic and things went even worse. We were treated badly by the front desk person and, despite trying to arrange this process for so long, we decided that this is not the right place to be. We then cancelled all the appointments we had with the clinic, and despite speaking to a second person that was better trained, there was no attempt to engage in trying to solve the problem we were having or to improve the experience or retain us as customers.

Shocking service, avoid at all costs - from utter lack of responsiveness to emails, inordinate wait time to speak to someone by phone, lack of follow-up to important and time sensitive requests for matters such as a request for PGA testing …
Thank you for taking the time to leave your feedback. We would like to discuss your experience further and invite you to contact us on feedback@crgh.co.uk

Although the doctors and medical staff here are professional, the communication from the clinic is extremely poor. Emails often go completely unanswered, phone calls take forever to be picked up, and you constantly have to chase them for …

I’ve been happy with the medical care I’ve received so far. The doctors and embryologists have been excellent, and I’ve felt well looked after on the clinical side. However, the administrative side of the clinic has been extremely frustrating and really detracts from the overall experience.

There are frequent administrative errors that you, as the patient, end up having to chase all while managing full-time work and the stress of IVF. Previously, the old portal system worked well, and messages were responded to quickly. Phone lines were accessible, and things felt manageable.

However, since the new system was introduced a month ago, things have gone significantly downhill. The portal is not user-friendly, messages sent through it are not received or responded to, and phone lines are so overwhelmed that I’ve waited 40–50 minutes multiple times without ever getting through. Emails are replied to, but often days later, which is unhelpful when you need to schedule time-sensitive scans or treatments.

I’ve raised my concerns via email a couple of times, but haven’t received a meaningful response. Most recently, a nurse failed to send a time-sensitive prescription, which required multiple follow-ups from me to resolve, without so much as an acknowledgment or apology.

In a recent nurse consultation I was reassured the portal is now working, but that just isn’t the case, unless they are just choosing to ignore messages that I have sent.

I’m already dreading having to contact CRGH to book in for a baseline scan. How am I meant to arrange something for the next 1-2 days when no one answers the phone, I can’t contact the nurses via portal and emails take at least 48 hours for a response. It’s an impossible system to navigate.

What’s most disappointing is the lack of communication. Given that the new system clearly isn’t working, there’s been no formal update or warning that portal messages are going unanswered. This level of disorganisation is simply not acceptable, especially considering the high costs of treatment.

To add to this, the finance team has made errors on contracts and charges in the past,
so invoices need to be checked carefully.

I understand that new systems can come with teething problems, but nearly a month on, there’s still no resolution, no timeframe for fixing the issues, and no accountability. Based solely on the administrative experience, I sadly cannot recommend the clinic at this time.

The doctors were very helpful and informative when you get the chance to talk to them. It is not easy to get a hold of the doctors if you have questions, the same goes for nurses. After going to another clinic we realised how indifferent …

We have had a very bad experience here. Reasons being: (1) the Dr needed to repeatedly be reminded of patient name and background, (2) No full blood profiling was taken before the cycle, (3) the Dr ignored the findings of a DNA fragmentation report we requested be carried out and (4) triggered too late. The one saving grace is the nurses and embryologists are all lovely and are competent in their jobs.

Edit: changing to 2 stars, following 2nd cycle.

My experience of egg freezing (one completed cycle) with CRGH has been mixed, …

The doctors at this clinic are excellent! Cannot fault them. But over the last 18 months we have experienced so many issues with the organisation of the clinic. Firstly, in 18 months we have never met our consultant face to face. Secondly, unless you self advocate for everything the nurses won't be aware what medication you are on / what protocol you are following. You need to constantly self-advocate or things are missed / happen incorrectly. It seems to be operating at NHS standards rather than private clinic standards now. Despite how much you pay...
Double check the cost of everything and get it in writing if not provided automatically!! …

Let me know our donor had pulled out via email so very impersonal.
When owed finance £600 over a 4 day period was called daily. However, when we were due our refund of over 12k it took them over 6 weeks to process? Was advised would be put on the priority list in sept and not heard a thing back from them since so realistically need to forget about having children. Doctors are good, the nursing team and some of the other admin. But very unpersonal, disjointed service received. Highly dissapointing

Communication is awful.  The admin team are clearly understaffed and it takes approx. one week to receive a reply.  They should understand that fertility treatment is a race against time and losing 1-2 months of treatment/attempts due to …

Not competitive on pricing and very opaque about costs, even when asked directly. Treatment costed double what I was told. I was also told that before work scan appointments wouldn’t be a problem. They were a significant problem. Therefore would not recommend.

I haven’t even started treatment yet, and I’m already seriously rethinking my choice. The clinic may have good medical reviews, but the communication and admin process have been extremely frustrating. …

We were discharged from the clinic with mixed feelings.

Negatives: My partner was sometimes treated in a way that made her feel stupid for asking simple questions, and we received conflicting information on several occasions. In one cycle, the wrong cycle’s notes were initially used, meaning we nearly skipped a cycle unnecessarily; we avoided this only through persistence. When problems arose, some staff focused on blaming colleagues rather than patient care. Telephone staff sometimes lacked basic awareness of the clinic’s own processes and did not take the time to read notes properly, which was extremely stressful when trying to get answers and book appointments at critical points in our cycles. Some nurses lacked compassion and sensitivity, leaving my partner in tears after two appointments. At certain points, senior staff admitted the clinic was overwhelmed with calls/emails, making it difficult to contact the clinic. Peaks and troughs happen, but continuing to advertise for new patients while being overwhelmed by existing ones felt like putting profit before people. Pharmasure’s delivery service is excellent (the pharmacy the clinic use), but their prices are extortionate: despite being owned by IBSA (the manufacturer of most of the prescribed drugs), we were quoted between 25–100% more than third-party suppliers for the same drugs. The process sometimes felt like a generic process rather than tailored diagnosis and treatment. Finance was too detached with invoices and chasers arriving at insensitive times. The clinic is busy and can feel like an IVF factory at times.

Positives: Some nurses - especially senior staff - were outstanding: kind, compassionate, responsive, and professional. We are particularly grateful to Jessica, Anca, Sue, and Libby, who went above and beyond. The lab staff were also empathetic, communicative, and professional. After we raised service issues, the clinic took steps to put things right, and we noticed a significant service improvement towards the end of our journey. Our consultant, Dr Wael Saab (one of two Dr Saabs at the clinic), was informative, listened to our questions, and provided good care. The portal resources were helpful. We became pregnant after two rounds of IVF (albeit it is early days), so the clinic delivered the desired outcome. We had confidence in the clinic via its results and reports from friends who are in the industry who vouched for the quality of the lab.

Verdict: We are grateful for the result, but there were too many avoidable service issues; I would be cautious recommending the clinic because it caused unnecessary stress at times, but we did get the desired result and I am aware that patients at competitor clinics report similar issues.

Some tips for other patients:

- Do pre-IVF blood tests early (as these can delay treatment).
- Start supplements early (ideally ~3 months before treatment). We took Ovum (women) and Proxeed (men).
- Order drugs early to avoid last minute ordering stress and cost.
- Use ASDA Pharmacy for IVF drugs: they offer IVF drugs at cost price, but do allow 3–5 days from order to collection (vs Pharmasure's overnight delivery service). Pls note ASDA require you to attend in person to order and collect with signed prescriptions + prescriptions need to be for whole boxes. We felt the extra hassle was worth the cost saving.
- Advocate for yourself: ask questions and escalate to senior staff if you are unsatisfied with the answers.
- Expect costs of ~£2–3k+ for testing, then £8k+ per cycle including drugs (2025 prices) - don't be caught out by the, much lower, headline price for a cycle that all clinics seem to use. The headline price does not include drugs, testing, and other standard procedures which are required.
- If male factor infertility is identified during initial testing, consider seeing a specialist andrologist/fertility urologist outside of the clinic - as fertility clinics generally seem to have more consultants with a gynaecology background, yet male factor infertility effects 50% of couples.

I am currently 8 day post transfer and today went to collect my prescription where the nurse had no idea about the quantity she should prescribe despite emailing and calling them yesterday to inform about the remaining medications which I …
I travelled a long way for the appointment (a simple blood test) and had to wait 40 minutes past my appointment time, no explanation or apology given. Hand hygiene was questionable. Disappointing.

I was supposed to do a egg freezing at CRHG. I did some tests at their clinic, and had a consultation call after. Everything felt very rushed, and not very personal. They said everything looked good and that I should start with the egg freezing process.

I then went to another clinic (Care Fertility) that I got recommended by a friend. They got to see my test results from CRHG, and informed me that I had PCOS which CRHG never said anything about. Care Fertility were so helpful, and personal.

The doctors and nurses at this place are amazing, very knowledgeable and by all metrics, acheive great results in terms of supporting people with fertility and genetic issues. However, the administrative structure of CRGH leaves a lot to be desired. You never know who you are meant to talk to to get information.

I had 3 rounds of NHS funded PGD IVF. I am sure my main consultant had my best interests at heart but she had a rather brusque manner and spoke extremely quickly all the time, as though she were always in a rush to get me out the door. In general the staff were not very approachable which saddens me as I need PGD and CRGH are very strong in this area. I wish I had had a different experience as I then could have then stayed with CRGH for these next rounds of privately funded PGD.

The doctors and nurses really care about their patients and will go above and beyond for you.  The finance team on the other hand are extremely aggressive and have no qualms whatsoever with issuing invoices at the eleventh hour and then …

We chose CRGH because it also conducted research.  We felt that this would mean they would be more update.  Unfortunately, it just meant that they had little 'bed side manner'.
I'll start off by saying that our two cycles were unsuccessful.  However, my negative opinion was formed during the first cycle.
Communication prior to implantation was excellent.
I was given a cervix relaxant several hours before implantation, there was no where comfortable to go through this, either the very small reception area or, as I chose, cured up in great pain on a chair in a corridor of the adjacent dental building.
I was not offered sedation for implantation, I struggle with smear tests but implantation was horrendous.
As is advised, I was expecting to lie on the trolley for a half an hour after implantation, but no, after two minutes I was sent elsewhere.
Second cycle, I thought I was pregnant, and their test confirmed this but at 7 weeks had a scan and they could not see anything.  Told that the better quality scan was not working.  Told to go back in a couple of weeks.  The feeling of being pregnant soon went.  Returned to clinic to be told very coldly that nothing there. I much later learned that I had probably had a chemical pregnancy.
For various reasons we chose not to do a third cycle with the final embryos.  After a couple of years of paying for them to be frozen we decided that we would donate them to anyone who needed embryos for pregnancy.  This was a complicated process.  We had to fill in many forms, including a heartfelt note to the 'future' child/ren at 18.  A couple of years after we were contacted by the clinic as they had omitted to do some essential medical tests.  When we went to the clinic again for an appointment, we were kept waiting a couple of hours, we didn't even have the appointment with the named person dealing with donation, but, in a tiny room.  All the forms had to be completed again.  On the floor of the 'office' were patient files, showing confidential information such as name and client number.  We were even left alone in the room with them.  We were expected to complete the forms in that cramped 'office'.  We didn't, we had to get out of there and do the forms justice.  We went to the nearby shopping plaza and did over a very very long coffee.
So for a couple of years, our embryos were not even available for another couple to use.  Heartbreaking.  Now, the regulations have changed and embryos need to be from a woman under 40 and who has had a live birth.  So now, my time with CRGH is finally coming to an end, after 10 years.

Would I have been happy if the IVF had been successful, yes of course.  Would I have still thought that the process at CRGH was poor, yes, absolutely.
